Review: A Mixed Experience at Parkdean Sandy Bay – Great Staff, but Misleading "All-Inclusive"**

3.5 I'd that was possible.

We recently returned from a 4-day stay at Parkdean Sandy Bay in Northumberland. While the location and staff were highlights, there were some significant points of frustration regarding the "All-Inclusive" package that future guests should be aware of.

The "All-Inclusive" Package: Misleading
The biggest disappointment was the "All-Inclusive" offering. In reality, this felt more like a restrictive Full Board than a true all-inclusive.
The Exclusions: No alcohol is included (which is understandable), but neither are starters, sides, or desserts.
The Limits: You are strictly limited to one item per meal. For example, you cannot order a sausage sandwich and a side of toast without being charged extra for the toast.
The Surcharges: Any main over £19.99 incurs an additional £5 charge.
Calling this "All-Inclusive" feels like a stretch and is potentially a case of false advertising.

Staff & Service
On a much more positive note, the staff in the Boathouse Restaurant were excellent. Joanne and Tammy (who served us all week for breakfast and lunch) were incredibly helpful, polite, and friendly. Joanne, in particular, was a total pleasure to talk to and made our dining experiences much brighter.

Dining & Food Quality
The food itself was actually very good:
Breakfast: A decent range of British and American cooked breakfasts and sandwiches.
Lunch: A limited menu (mostly sandwiches and wraps), but everything we tried—from the Southern Fried Chicken wrap to the Churrasco Steak—was tasty.
Evening: A fair variety of pies, curries, and grills. Pizzas were unavailable for the first half of our stay, which was a shame, but the quality of the other mains was consistent.

The Accommodation
The caravan was overall nice and well-maintained.
Cleanliness: Generally good, though some dust remained on shelves and cupboard tops.
Facilities: The shower was a standout—clean, spacious, with great water pressure and heat.
Tech: While Freeview was available, the advertised Netflix and Prime apps were unusable due to poor signal/connection.
The View: We were treated to a lovely view of the coastline and rapeseed fields, and the park grounds were kept very clean.

Local Area & Attractions
The location is very convenient for supplies, being only a 10-minute drive from Asda, Aldi, and Lidl. We also highly recommend a trip to Lilidorei at The Alnwick Garden (about 30 mins away)—it is fantastic if you are traveling with children.

Final Verdict: Sandy Bay is a clean park with fantastic staff and good food, but Parkdean needs to be much more transparent about what "All-Inclusive" actually means. We enjoyed our stay, but felt the meal plan was poorly communicated.

The first night I woke up busting for the toilet and it was locked. Having to hold it till the morning till they unlocked was very difficult. The next day I got the code to the male toilet. The only problem is I woke up busting for the toilet and got to the toilet and it's 2am and the pathetic old push button lock doesn't open when I put the code in. I was busting so bad and trying to open the pathetic old lock which after about 10 trya of putting in the exact code I was given I had to once again be locked out of the toilet again. I am just glad I didn't need to do a number 2 otherwise I would have just had to do it at the bathroom door. Instead I was forced to go back to my tent where they had also just turned on the sprinklers so holding it in wasn't possible anymore. With way to many guests there was no where to pee. Truely a 3rd world experience when it comes to their bathrooms being locked with locks so crappy they don't even open with the supplied code. So I had to walk out to the beach in the middle of the night just to find somewhere to pee before my bladder almost burst. I almost stepped on a baby smake on the beach path too. For the discomfort, danger and inconvenience it caused me I had no choice but to right a review to warn others bring your own toilet or suffer having no toilet access all night. What sort of a fool locks the bathroom at night so the guests cant use it? Then to have such a crappy old analogue push button lock that doesn't open even when you put the exact pin code in.
It was really a very bad thing to do to the guests.

I didn’t take any photos of the room, but it was absolutely stunning. Waking up and looking up at the high ceiling with its gorgeous wooden beams was such a peaceful way to start the day.

The location is ideal — close to the beach and just a short drive to the shops. A perfect spot for a relaxing and fun escape.

Review of an Excellent Stay at a Clean and Well-Maintained Caravan Park

Overview

My recent stay at the caravan park was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment I arrived, I was impressed by the cleanliness and maintenance of the facilities. The park is designed to provide a serene and exclusive experience, with a limited number of sites that enhance privacy and comfort.

Cleanliness and Maintenance

One of the standout features of this caravan park is its impeccable cleanliness. The grounds are well-kept, with manicured lawns and tidy pathways that invite you to explore. The amenities, including restrooms and communal areas, are regularly cleaned and maintained, ensuring a pleasant environment for all guests. This attention to detail reflects the management’s commitment to providing a high-quality experience.

Helpful Staff

The staff at the caravan park were incredibly helpful throughout my stay. They greeted me warmly upon arrival and were always available to answer questions or provide assistance. Whether it was offering recommendations for local attractions or helping with any issues related to my accommodation, their friendly demeanor made me feel welcome and valued as a guest.

Accommodation Options

The majority of sites in the park consist of self-contained units or permanent caravans, which adds to the overall comfort of the stay. With only 9 shady caravan sites and 4 camping spots available, it creates an intimate atmosphere that feels exclusive compared to larger parks. This setup allows guests to enjoy their space without feeling crowded, making it ideal for families or individuals seeking a peaceful getaway.

Proximity to Beaches

One of the best aspects of this caravan park is its proximity to stunning beaches. Just a short 150-meter walk leads you to clean white sandy shores with turquoise waters. This easy access allows guests to enjoy beach activities such as swimming, sunbathing, or simply taking leisurely strolls along the coast. The combination of beautiful scenery and convenient access makes this location truly special.

Conclusion

Overall, my experience at this caravan park exceeded my expectations in every way. The cleanliness, helpful staff, exclusive accommodation options, and proximity to beautiful beaches create an ideal setting for relaxation and enjoyment. I would highly recommend this caravan park to anyone looking for a tranquil escape in nature while still enjoying modern comforts.

Just spent 4 nights as 4 adults, an under 2 & a dog for a Parkdean Tots Break. The break overall was great value for money including paying extras for the dog and a high chair; we selected the Willerby caravan which was dog friendly. Next time I’d select a larger caravan as it was a bit of a tight squeeze for us all but more than sufficient for the time we spent in it. The lounge sofa and carpet could do with a deep clean, being a dog friendly van I can understand why it was grubby but the sofa was visibly dirty and this was a little disappointing. Cannot fault the cleanliness and condition of the rest of the van though. The site is brilliant, tidy, gorgeous views and some fantastic facilities including the kids playground, swimming pool and show bar. We enjoyed several meals in The Boathouse which were good value and the staff were excellent, often balancing serving at the bar, bringing out food and cleaning up, all done with a smile and friendly chats. Our little one loved swimming twice during our stay, lifeguards were fab, and length of the session was just enough. The changing facilities are a bit small though so get ready as much as you can at your caravan. The activities and entertainment on offer was brilliant however our toddler was possibly just a bit too young to enjoy it as much as other children, some activities like the disco/morning sessions he enjoyed but overall we feel he’ll enjoy it more nearer pre-school age. This however doesn’t negatively reflect how much this park has on offer and how hard the staff work. Overall we had a brilliant stay at Sandy Bay and wouldn’t hesitate to recommend it to others.

Caravan was not the expected one we booked its spec level was dramatically lower. It was old and tired and small with no where to put a cot for the baby it was booked for.
The heating system was broken so it was on even in 20 degree weather. The park had very little on site for kids, the pool was small, the soft play just a padded square with not much to engage in and beach access near impossible with prams and small children.

Good access to local towns via cycle path, aquarium and funfair 20-25 minute drive.

Great Parkdean park !!     Shout out who stood out to us on our trip was a lovely young lad Nathan who works at the amusements, hard working and very polite, and Meg the children’s entertainment !!!     This is the 2nd time I have mentioned in my feed back and when I asked Nathan if he’s got this feed back he didn’t no what I was saying   As am a frequent visitor to your sight,  your staff should have this feed back

Lovely well kept park. Super pet friendly accommodation that our labrador thought of as a home from home. It was very clean, comfortable and spacious with care taken to provide you with most things you would need for a relaxing stay. The beach was brilliant although not all that easy to access but great for dogs and there is an indoor swimming pool for children. Newbiggin is worth a visit although that beach does have dog restricted areas (not too well signposted but advised by a lovely local gentleman). All in all a very relaxing break and looking to return soon.

Really disappointing first experience here at the Sandy bay bar. I ordered a child meal and before I paid I realised it was put through wrong on the till as it should have been a tots meal. Apparently in the time of the price coming up on the till to it being cancelled (hadn’t even paid) it had been ‘made’ and was on the plate ready to be served. This is apparently my fault and was expected to pay the extra. How on earth can something be cooked in that time and then blame me even before it had been paid for. Mistakes happen but the attitude was unnecessary.
Location is great, and not far to travel for entertainment. Laundry out of service, chippy and food van opening timings was a hit or miss. Caravan was clean, but furniture had seen better days.
Best Park dean park out of the lot fantastic site fantastic service and the entertainment staff are spot on
Lovely location poor food and staff some were lovely some weren't definitely need more training with people skills but overall nice caravan and lovely walks etc

The caravan was clean and tidy, which was a good start. However the beds were quite small, even for the kids and the quality was disappointing. The overall cleanliness of the park was just ok, but unfortunately there was a quite a bit of dog mess which was off putting. Entertainment was lacking or it's none, especially considering the price paid. No WiFi or phone signal in the park. If you are simply looking for a place to stay in caravan, it might be suitable but it's also depending on your allocated unit. Overall it didn't feel like good value for money

Don't know how they do it for the price.
Food great room very every clean
Don't know how many times we have been will be back in Sept pool  spot on  the guy in the pool really good nice to see it at this high standard thanks.
Direct access to a beautiful beach. Well maintained site. Friendly staff.
after several visits to reception to report a problem with the tv , no person came to resolve our issue.

Last minute booking for 4 day stay in a caravan.  Excellent customer service dealing with the booking. Lovely caravan close to centre, contained everything we needed.  Wee problem with smell in lounge area (smoke) (All caravans and decks are no smoking areas - but not everyone adheres to this). Also we couldn't figure out how to work the heating.  Lovely reception staff got on to this and matters were quickly resolved.  Magic machine in lounge area for 90 minutes or so got rid of the odour, heating was a simple fix.  All staff are friendly and make you feel welcome.

Food and drink on offer is good and there are lots of offers e. two trad breakfast £12.99 (normally £8.49 each), selected mains two for £12.

Lots to do on site especially if you have kids.  Locally many places to visit - Newbigging by the Sea, Amble both nice seaside towns (more to do, see amd eat in Amble, )Woodhorn Mining Museum well worth a visit.

All in all we enjoyed our stay.  Thanks again to all the staff.

Overall, a family friendly venue. However, there are some downfalls. The prices within the Boathouse Bar are excessive, although the food is of good quality, the prices listed are more than I would be willing to pay in the future.
The cleanliness inside our caravan was inadequate, the shower had not been cleaned. There was hair from previous holiday goers still in the shower, stains on the carpet in the bedroom, cobwebs hanging from the ceiling inside the bedroom, damage to the internal walls in the bedroom, damage to the lower left en-suite bathroom door, stained radiators within the bedroom and dirt/food crumbs still under the table which have not been cleaned. I had to take it upon my self to use anti-bacterial wipes inside the shower since it was not cleaned additionally, I had to sweep the kitchen floor due to this also not being done.

On the other hand, the staff couldn’t have been friendlier in and around the park.

Overall, I would unfortunately not stay here again as cleanliness is a detrimental part to my overall comfort when on holiday.
Brilliant value for money. Entertainment was great for the children and the venue was very clean
Had a great time getting out of ste was a rigmarole make you drive all the was round site for no apparent reason

Been going sandy bay on and off many years now , and haven't been for 2 years untill this weekend ,we booked for my son's birthday ...
So we paid a big lump extra to get sea view ..I was really disappointed when we arrived as you could only see part of the sea view as caravans wer in the way so that wasn't sea view to us !
Caravan clean and nice , the staff are lovely and security also ...
Food not great .. so expensive and the drinks have really shot up since 2 years back ...breakfast the toast was stone cold as was bacon and scrambled egg ,
The night times to me personally the best entertainment was the Sunday night when hardly anyone was in which was a shame as it was good ..and the best night from Friday and Saturday when was more busy ...
The new chicken van we went one evening at 925 pm we ordered then was told sorry no bread buns , the fish shop was closed also ..things close really early considering amount people coming from clubs on a night !
Lovely clean park as always but amount of years ive been going back 25 years even  it was amazing then the  club was fantastic . And fish shop was open late and served good old fashioned fish n chips ...
Overall we won't be in a hurry to come back , as the prices are way too high for standards you receive .
Little break away after my son's sats at school ,great location, private van rental...close to coast for walks ...plenty of activities

An amazing family orientated holiday so much to do staff go above and beyond to make your holiday amazing 👏 having an autistic 3 year old the staff helped so much to give him an amazing holiday highly recommend this place 😀
Beautiful place
Immaculate clean caravan lovely weather fab weekend
First time at this destination but it won't be the last! A great place to stay.

Got a sun holiday here, didn’t know what to expect with it being so cheap. But fab place! Upgraded to a brand new caravan for free, gorgeous beaches and coastline. We could lock the kids on the decking around the caravan and know they’re safe. Lots of free entertainment, fun for kids, clean swimming pool and facilities and all the staff were great. We had a lovely break and would recommend.

Close to Ashington  town ,  rented caravan from booking.com website although lady at the customer service was helpful as we are first time to caravan experience. Beach was lovely and quite right next to  park.  On site restaurant was alright with decent choice of food and drink.

My Wife and I have just returned from a 5 day holiday trip here. I honestly wish we could have stayed another week. The location is easy enough to get to by car, not sure about public transport. Upon arrival, we were greeted at the reception by a very friendly and cheerful lady who made our booking in easy. She gave us a map of the site and showed us our caravan (404) easy to drive to with ample parking available. Our caravan was on the 1st row towards the car parking area so we didn't encounter too much mud.

Upon getting in, we were greeted with a lovingly sized caravan, beautifully presented and very clean. Has everything you need from plates to utensils, brushes and iron. A big fridge/freezer, plenty of storage space. You can instantly feel the comfort upon entering. We had a look around the caravan, 2 very clean toilets, a walk in shower, a lovely double bed in 1 room and 2 single beds in another room. The heating worked great and it was very comfortable with lots of lights to help create a warm atmosphere. You even turn up or down the brightness if you want.

The double bed was very spacious and very comfortable! Had 5 great nights sleep. Very clean sheets with plenty of spares.

There are plenty of ammendments on site from fish and chips to arcades and launderette. A shop too. The restaurant on site is lovely and clean. The staff were very pleasant and friendly. Prices were fair too. We didn't attend the events on site but we played alot of arcades. The arcade staff were very helpful and friendly throughout our visit.

The site overall is very maintained with absolutely no rubbish or trash anywhere. Very quiet and cosy.

The only, only gripe I have is the WiFi is very poor inside and mobile data is really poor around the area so consider that if you decide to come to stay. The WiFi towers outside are great when you are outside but in the caravans, we didn't get any signal at all and gave up trying to connect. The caravan had an RJ45 LAN port but didn't seem to work when I tried linking it to the smart TV inside.

Overall we would definitely come back as we loved every single minute of it

5*

Edit (28th April 2025 - 2nd May 2025) We came back to SandyBay for another 5 days as we enjoyed our 1st holiday here. We were given the exact same caravan as last year (404) which was reallyfunny. Still equally as nice as last year. Caravan clean and maintained just as before. Parkdean have upgraded the WiFi on-site as I now get WiFi inside the caravan so that's a bonus. Had trouble last year with the WiFi which was a minor inconvenience. That has been rectified. We had some food on-site on our 1st day which was a let down. The fish and chips I ordered were nice but my partner's Haloumi, chips and coleslaw were disappointing. She said the Haloumi was dry, chips were cold and coleslaw looked like the cabbage was rotting slightly. Took over an hour from order to getting our food, and it wasn't busy either. We didn't go back after our 1st day.

The arcades have changed a bit, still plenty to play but the 2p machine prizes aren't as good this time around.

There is a new burger eatery on-site called Thunderbird Chicken. We unfortunately didn't get a chance to try as when we fancied trying, it wasn't open.

Everything else is exactly as before which is highly appreciated. So overall, still 5* and we'll definitely come back again. Hopefully get 404 again for the 3rd time.
Cheap caravan stay but entertainment was poor. All inhouse. Kids were bored. Hour long bingo each night. Hot and miss whether the takeaways were open. No ice cream/ waffle  shop all week.
Clean well placed and a nice beach

The caravan we originally booked was definitely not the caravan we were given. It was no where near extra wide. There was a dirty sock underneath my son's bed upon entering the accommodation and a shelf was missing from the fridge meaning storing milk was difficult. We have stayed in many parks across the UK and this is probably one of the worst for the accomodation.
Thoroughly enjoyed

I can't say on the rooms as I stayed in a family owned caravan, however the kids really enjoy going here and when the sun is out it is actually a really good day out walking the coastal walk and spending a day in the town and enjoying the local scenery and pubs witch all of what I experienced was welcoming. However if you are in desperate need of supplies the shop on site is small but dose hold a good selection of what you need, the boat house is a nice place to chill out however always look for somewhere local aswell I always say. Swimming pool is grate and staff amazing with kids, entertainment is good but some days is just good enjoying good old family time in the van.

Fantastic little holiday park. I've been here a couple of times now and have booked for this summer. The park sits right next to a stunning beach.
The caravans have always been immaculate and have everything you need. I have always found the staff to be very friendly and accommodating too.
The on-site facilities are adequate (the arcade and pool are quite small) but there is plenty on offer to keep everyone entertained.
The evening entertainment is always amazing.
There is plenty to do and explore locally also.  recommend a trip to the Ocean beach pleasure park or Northumberland zoo.
The staff here are absolutely first class. They always go the extra mile to help and assist everyone. Staff like that are the icing on the cake. Thank you.
Lovely place and great service at the ice cream place from Brady,lovely smile, quick service and added to the holiday experience x

I will never come back again and no wounder people are selling there caravans.
This is a 2 star Park that once used to be a 4 star.

Excellent park in a super area. Very clean and tidy. Staff welcoming and friendly. Lodge was large and comfortable.

Perfect weekend everyone was so friendly and amazing thanks so much

The main thing is the location of the park. It's clean, quiet enough for a family get away. We tried one of the food options and it was terrible. So good we will be going back

Excellent short break, booked caravan privately via Airbnb and it was the best one I ever stayed in. The park itself was tidy and clean. Somewhat expensive but aren’t they all. Would definitely stay again
4 night spring break to sandy bay, staff polite wherever we went, good onsite bar if a little expensive, caravan spacious and clean. Good location explored Alnwick amble newbeggin Morpeth Ashington
Just went for a look round looks great

Thunderbird Fried Chicken was amazing.
I walked through it, I know nothing more

Beautiful place. Beautiful scenery.  The caravan park was clean and quiet. Caravan was very clean. Staff were brilliant.  Would definitely go here again .

Love this place

the beach and the area is lush

Stayed in one of there lodges was spotless and had some decking which was good as we had the dog with us and he sat outside enjoying the sun as weather was good .We chose to eat and have some drinks a couple of days the staff Mandy and Debbie we're really nice and made us feel very welcome so shout out to them see you next year girls !! Xx
Accomodation excellent,  clean and tidy. Location perfect for visting our selected trips

Caravan is lovely, park and football court is good for kids. Pool is an ok size, beach is very nice. Amusements is very small. Staff in the entertainment are great as is the entertainment. Drinks are over priced. We have been going out the site for food but decided to try the restaurant for today. Menu changes on a Sunday to limited options. The kids meals are very small and the Sunday dinners are horrendous! The only thing nice on the whole plate were the puddings which were definitely out of a packet and not home made. Mash was like water and a tiny portion, gravy also like water, veg was tinned peas which were fine then just parsnips and carrots, awful and solid 'we were told there wasn't any carrots available for the kids meal but after how hard they were she wouldn't of ate them anyway' .. My 8 year old could of done a better job with the roasties, think they were cooked yesterday 'hardly' then warmed in the microwave for today. We both got the beef and was awful, very dry and chewy. Highly disappointed with all the food and it cost us over £75! Leaving all 4 of us still hungry afterward. Definitely will not be going there again! Havent tried the fish shop yet, think we will stick to eating out of the site from now on. Luckily theres McDonald's close by aswel a few other places which also deliver to the site. I would come again as the site is nice for a short break but definitely will not be eating in the restaurant again!
The caravan was clean on arrival and was well equipped. The park was clean and had good things to do.

Lovely kept park, although small but there's direct access to the beach and not far from the town centre. Caravan was lovely and clean. Ate out a couple of nights in the restaurant, it was a little pricey but food was lovely. We were served by a lovely girl called Kacie she was very helpful and always smiling. Entertainment was good, really enjoyed the bingo. We will be back next year.

Visited for Tots Break with our 3 year old and 9 month old. We've been to several before but our first time at Sandy Bay.

Caravan was absolutely disgusting. The floors had definitely not been cleaned. Haribo, make up, coins and beads left under the beds, not to mention the huge amount of dust.

I kept having to move little bits of dirt/food/plastic to the side out of his reach and they were still there days later. Activities were great and kids enjoyed.

Swimming pool and lifeguards were great.

All staff were helpful.

Restaurant and food were great. Bit slow at times but was fine. Soft play was nice to have there.

We won't return. Too disgusting
Looking to buy a caravan went to a few places come back here great location easy access 👍

It's a nice place for a vacation. The bedrooms are tiny but are reasonably comfortable. The sandy beaches and the views from the coast are a major attraction and make this place one of the best to visit in England.
Amazing Beach for our pooches.. it was having the beach within a five minute walk that made this holiday. The staff were excellent so very friendly the accommodation wasn't great if I am honest.

Good site, with access to a small beach. Ideal to walk the dog.  Good on site facilities and friendly staff.
Stayed in a friend's caravan which was on the front line with incredible sea views.  There's so much to see in both Northumberland and county Durham

It would be 5 stars but the food while nice was priced more than it really should be.

Our first visit to Sandy Bay and a Park Dean site, it won't be our last!!
Absolutely Spotless! There were always staff cleaning (Mandy and Diane i think) Our booking was changed without us knowing but the lady on reception (think her name was Abby) Sorted it out before we arrived and upgraded us to a lodge which we didn't expect and appreciated.
We would definitely return to Sandy Bay and book a lodge.
The beach was easy accessible, especially with young kids. Easily got the pushchair down and back up. Beautiful beaches!
The pool was a good size and clean.
We ate in the restaurant on our last night. The soft play are was really good for our 2 little ones. Food was really nice and fast service. Staff are really friendly.
The playground was really good for our 3 year old.
The Shop had everything we needed and friendly staff.
Nice little amusements too.

Would definitely recommend!
Thankyou for having us!
Great when weather is good held to ransom with on site facilities well over priced

On arrival we found the caravan not to a very high standard of cleanliness, the curtain was dirty, there was grease on the splash back, the carpet was smelly and nor cleaned around the edges, also the sofa was heavily stained. We sorted it ourselves rather than having to wait around for cleaning team to come round. The site is very small, all the site features are located in one central building meaning its all crammed in. The shop is really tiny so make sure you bring all the bits you need with you. The swimming pool was absoloutly not fit for purpose, there was only 6 changing rooms and it was very un-hygienic. The entertainment hall is pretty big considering and was typical of a park dean. The restaurant was usless as they had no pizzas available. Overall its a great base for exploring the local area and beach access but its very run down, small and not very clean. Wont be coming back to sandy bay unfortunately, will stick to our usual parkdean site Skipsea sands, of which Sandybay should be looking at for an example on how to run a good parkdean site. I forgot to mention the views are amazing.

Location was ok, park was grubby and accommodation dirty.  We complained and they sent one lady to clean two caravans.  The beds had to be all cleaned and changed, the lounge seats were all stained with goodness knows what.  The oven was filthy and general grubbiness round the caravans.  Cigarette butts outside the caravan, we do not smoke. Park dean were not interested. Even when I complained when I got home they were still uninterested, got your money… tough.  Was the response.  Such a shame as we spent a lot of money in the restaurant, bar etc as there was a few of us.  We will definitely it be back and due to the customer service of park dean they will be avoided as well.

Addition….. Parkdean have replied to this review, however I would like to add the situation was not rectified.  The caravan was still dirty.  I had to do my best to clean it myself.  Park dean were not interested or upholding my complaint when I went through their complaints department.  So yes Parkdean it does deter me from visiting anymore of their parks and giving them my hard earned money!!! Or recommending them to anyone.  Awful park! Awful service from Parkdean, dismissive and uninterested!
Basic caravan park

We arrived on August 26, 2024, at 4:20 PM, and were horrified by the condition of our caravan (Booking Reference: 11279021).

Issues we encountered:

Unclean Toilets: The toilets had clearly not been cleaned.
Dirty Kitchen and Cookware: The kitchen and cooker were filthy, and the utensils were also unclean.
Stained and Worn-Out Mattress: The main bedroom mattress was stained, had food crumbs on it, and was sagging in the middle—completely unacceptable.
Soiled and Smelly Sofa: The sofa was heavily stained with urine and emitted a horrible smell, making it unusable.
We immediately reported these issues to the reception and reached out to online support due to the unbearable urine smell. Unfortunately, we were just redirected back to reception with no real help offered.

After a long wait, the mattress was replaced, and the cleaners used bleach on the toilet and kitchen, but the sofa area remains disgusting—still stained and reeking of urine. We were told no further cleaning could be arranged until tomorrow morning.

Considering cancelling the rest of our stay now. It's simply unacceptable to be expected to stay in such unsanitary conditions that pose a health risk.

This was our first time at Parkdean, and it’s likely our last. We expected much better standards of cleanliness and customer service.

Update:

I noticed in other reviews that Parkdean encourages guests to report issues during their stay. We checked in today and promptly posted this review to highlight the serious concerns we've encountered. We are hopeful that Parkdean will take our feedback seriously and address these issues swiftly.

We’re giving Parkdean the opportunity to make things right. If they resolve the situation satisfactorily, I’ll be happy to update this review with a positive outcome. Otherwise, we may have no choice but to cancel the remainder of our holiday.

Having read many reviews online, I was worried the place might be dirty and run down.
Although not brand new, the park is generally clean and tidy, with friendly, helpful staff. Access to the beach is superb. The pool is indoor, heated and well maintained.  Prices for food and drink are perhaps a pound or two more expensive per head than other establishments outside of the complex (isn't this part of the business model of a holiday park?), but there are deals to be had and if you use the park as a base from which to go out and explore surrounding attractions, it doesn't really matter anyway.
My family and my self loved this resort was brilliant

Cannot fault the caravan. Clean upon arrival. Beds exceeded expectations (which were modest). Grass around the park is well kept. However,  they have a significant litter / refuse problem which needs addressing. The inside "entertainment " .....well, to be fair I never got past the entrance.  Having seen inside,  I could see the carpets were plastered with litter. Kids will of course make a mess, but it was way beyond that. It was quite horrendous. We excluded this "entertainment " from our stay and used the location as a base to explore the area. I guess I'd do that again,  but I think I'd stop short of recommending.
Amazing beach but resort facilities were absolutely horrible
Nice place to stay
Security and staff need lessons on how to deal and understand problem children they are so arrogant
Love the caravan and the site. The boat house only has quarter menu on every Sunday and very expensive £60 for 2 cheese bacon burger& fries and a ceaser salad plus 3 pints.

Very good value.
Lovely spacious caravan.
Good base for visiting nearby places.
Friendly helpful staff.

Very good holiday park, friendly staff who go above and beyond to make your holiday a fantastic holiday.
Facilities are spotless too.
The shop is very well stocked and the items are reasonably priced.
We have been four times to Sandy Bay and have just booked up for June 2025.
Can't wait.

Was nice and quiet clean and tidy caravan only down fall is that they kept closing the bar  when watching football so had to go alway through other bar even tho there was more in the food part  let it down food was very expensive nearly £20 for spagetti £2 for one slice of bread with butter
Location was beautiful.

Leaving Sandy Bay today can't see us coming back Caravan was a private let and was stunning the  park is really run down now old caravans dumped in the middle of the site decking lying left dumped pot holes on the road the restaurant prices are far to Expensive and very little choice Haven has a lot better variety , very slow service especially when it's not busy no excuse for the drink prices these  are ridiculous excellent views of the river and North Sea

Absolutely beautiful location on the beach. Amazing part of the country with loads to see and do. The site itself is a typical Parkdean, good but not great facilities and a little pricey but you don't have to use them with Newbiggen so close.
Superb site. Clean tidy, organised. Cafe is super class. Amenities 10/10

Thank you Sandy Bay we will be coming back
Great range of drinks within the show bar, really good staff brought my order to my table for me and was really pleasant.
Nice place to stay.

We've stayed here loads of times and always have a lovely time. Caravans are clean and tidy and have everything you need for a nice break. Also not far to Newbiggin or Ashington. My car was off the road so used Phoenix Taxis app. Always spot on time and decent prices. We will be back x

Very large holiday park if like me a foot soldier it was a long walk to the reception check in the entertainment was good

Sandy Bay - Park Dean is an ok caravan park. But here are the NEGATIVES and POSITIVES to help people decide if it is the right park for them:

** NEGATIVES **

1) Many of the caravans DO NOT have a concrete parking space at the side of the caravan, so several small car parks are scattered around the park. This means that you may need to haul your luggage over the grassed areas that surround each caravan, which could be wet and muddy if it's been raining. Many of caravans have quite a bit of space between them, so it's a great shame that the park has not provided each caravan with its own parking space. Driving over the grassed areas is not permitted, but some people did it and I don't blame them.

2) The roads that guide you to the various zones and exits is a bit crazy. The road markings are worn out in some areas and seem wrongly placed, resulting in driver confusion. The one way system is a pain as getting to the exit from my caravan involved a 1-2 mile tour of the park! If I broke the rules and drove just 100 meters the wrong way I would have reached the exit in about 15 seconds! Not only would I have reached the exit quicker, I would have saved 1-2 miles worth of petrol and reduced environmental pollution! Something the park should consider.

3) The caravan was nice and modern, however, the seating in the lounge looked a little dirty and smelt a bit. But a quick blast with some Antibacterial Fabric Spray sorted the smell and any hygiene issues overnight.

4) The shower head mounting pole in the caravan was broken and falling to bits, so getting the shower head to stay in position was difficult.

5) The double bedroom in my 8 berth extra wide caravan was very cramped, with only about 10 inches each side of the bed. This made getting in and out of bed quite difficult.

6) The bathroom in my caravan was ok, but it had a 6 to 7 inch deep cabinet directly above the sink, so it was easy to whack your head on it when having a wash!

7) Sandy Bay is not a good area if you have a mobile phone that uses the Vodafone or Asda Mobile network, as the signal is poor. It's seems ok for O2 though. With Free Wi-Fi on-site this should not be a problem anyway.

8) Many of the tourist attractions (National Trust, Angel of the North, Beamish Museum, Alnwick Castle) are a fair distance away from Sandy Bay, so be prepared to drive upto 40 miles if you plan to visit them.

** POSITIVES **

1) When I arrived my caravan was ready. The woman working in the small reception cabin was nice and friendly. She gave me my keys and plotted a route on a paper map to show my caravans location.

2) The WHOLE of the caravan park has Wi-Fi access. You need to create an account, but once done, you have the option to have a FREE 2 Mbps service or a much faster service for a fee. You may need to log into your account daily to keep using the free service, but this takes seconds to do.

3) My caravan (8 berth extra wide) was modern and of good standard. It had everything I needed, including a good sized shower, 2 toilets, good heating system with heated bathroom towel rail, a 32" TV with DVD player, and a full sized 7ft tall fridge/freezer!

4) The park is located as near to the sea as it could possibly be! It also has a good beach for walking, but be very careful of the tide as it comes all the way in leaving little sand.

5) By looking at the provided map, the onsite facilities include a swimming pool, fish & chip shop, amusement arcade, showbar, bar & restaurant, Nisa store, launderette, outdoor sports court, and ice cream stall (near beach). However, I did not use or explore any of these, so, cannot comment on them.

6) The park was fairly peaceful and quiet during my stay.

We visited this site for a weeks holiday and booked what was classed as a good quality pet friendly van. Prior to our visit we received a phone call from Parkdean asking if we wanted to postpone our holiday due to the inclement weather that was being experianced, we declined and they said they may have to relocate us within the site, we asked for confirmation that the van would be the same quality and was assured it would be. When we arrived we found that you could not park adjacent to the van but had to park on the hard standing about 25 yards from the van, no problem there except that there was no pathway to the van meaning you had to walk through soft ground to the van. The van was adequate but we got the feeling we were the first visitors to this van this season, it was clean and the heating was running. We then found that the tap in the kitchen which is supposed to swing left and right was absolutely rigid, when we went to use the frying pan my wife had to wash it out as it was thick with grease, next morning the heating did not come on and upon inspection and my knowledge of this type of heating I found that the system was not pressurised so I had to fix that. The TV system that they now use is fully digital inso much as everything is run from their WiFi system, no problem there all worked ok, however, the TV was a Smart TV that had iPlayer Netflix etc. but you had no access to that unless you paid to get their WiFi at around £29 per week (I pay less than that for a months high speed unlimited at home!) You can ge free WiFi but it is very slow and kicks you off about every hour or so. Overall we were very dissapointed with this visit, when we have visited Parkdean sites previously a senior member of staff has normally come to the van to check if all is ok, no one did on this occasion, when we checked out normaly again they say 'was everthing OK?' again not at this site, just 'Checking out? Goodbye' I do not see how a van that has a fault like the sink tap was not reported by a member of the cleaning staff so it could be fixed prior to arrival, I did not bother reporting it because it would be a major job for someone to come and fix it and I was not prepared to waste my holiday whilst waiting for the repair to be done. Overall I cannot reccomend this site, the area is great but I would not go back to this site again, very dissapointed with their service overall.

We are on a four night break at Sandy Bay Holiday Park in Northumberland with our granddaughter who has a number of health problems.
All went well until the 3rd evening, when on returning to the caravan we found that we had no heating or hot water . After contacting out of hours number it was identified that we had run out of gas. This on a cold wet March night.
Fortunately for us, Wayne Bell the security Manager was working that evening, going over and above what he was meant to do. He managed to get a new gas bottle and connected this himself, getting his van bogged down in the awful mud on site, in the process. Thank you so much, Wayne, otherwise we would have had to left the caravan to find warmer accommodation for our granddaughter. This is due to her medical conditions that mean being cold and unable to be kept clean, would have put her at significant risk.
I was not happy with the response of the reception team the following morning, as despite me trying to explain, they were totally dismissive of the situation.
Wayne also came back the following evening to check on granddaughters welfare, cannot thank him enough for his help and compassion.

We've just had a 4 night stay at Sandy bay. It's getting into out of season so we naturally had a different experience than if we'd stayed in summer. You need to pay for this to access your own netflix etc in the caravan however you can register upto 10 devices so we thought it was worth it. The staff around the resort are super friendly and helpful. We went to reception a few times for some info.

Amenities:
The soft play is small and the flooring is damaged. The foam underneath where it's been 'repaired' with tape is coming loose which is a choking risk to smaller children. I hope they get this fixed for their next season.

There's 2 cash machines onsite but they charge to use them.
There's a small arcade, we had a go on the 2p slot machines and we got tons of sweeties, they paid out well.

The launderette is £4.70 for a wash and £3.70 for the drier..some machines were broken but seemed to be fixed quickly.

There is a pool but it was quite busy while we were there. You have to book in advance.
We did fancy fish & chips for our last night but the chippy on site is shut on a Tues, weds and Thurs so if you're there midweek you can't use it which is a shame. The onsite shop is very overpriced. I went to look for nappies and they sell a small pack of pampers for £6!

The nearest village is cramlington which has an Aldi, Sainsbury's with an Argos, Asda and small retail park. We went there for groceries. There's tons of free things to do nearby. Northumberlandia is worth a visit to burn off kids energy. We went to Newcastle center one day to 7stories a children's book museum which easily kills a few hours. Parking nearby on street is super cheap, 60p per hour.

The sea is really close and you can hear the waves crashing which is soothing.
We didn't use the restaurant onsite because there's so many bad reviews about the food. We looked at a few menus when the kids used the softplay and the menus on the tables hadn't been wiped clean and were dirty.

There's lots of planned kids activities to book and we were given a guide when we checked in. However I do think some were overpriced. £8 to do pumpkin carving for example is a lot for families on a budget.

We saved our leftover bread and crackers and took the kids to the pond onsite to feed the ducks instead. The outdoor play area is very good too.

It's a great base to explore nearby areas and wella maintained. I didn't see any litter during our stay and the grounds were kept well.

Nachdem wir nun schon mehrfach in Mobilheimen auf unterschiedlichen Parkdean Ressorts gewesen sind (und dabei immer sehr zufrieden waren), hat es uns diesmal zur Sandy Bay geführt.

Erster Eindruck: eine Großstadt aus Mobilheimen, die sich dicht aneinander reihen.
Die sonstige Ausstattung (Shop, Snackbar, Spielhalle, Veranstaltungsräume und Veranstaltungen) entsprechen dem, was wir schon von anderen Ressorts kannten und sind durchweg in Ordnung.

Bewertungen im Detail:
Mobilheim:
Die Treppe am Eingang war nicht richtig befestigt und vermittelte kein sicheres Gefühl beim Betreten des Mobilheims.
Leider gab es auf einem der Betten Haare und Gras. Ein frisches Bett stellen wir uns anders vor.
Eine dicke Staubschicht auf dem elektrischen Kamin zeugt ebenfalls davon, dass eine ordentliche Grundreinigung hier nicht stattgefunden hat.
Beim Einschalten des Kamins verbreitet sich sofort ein übler Geruch im Raum, eine Nutzung war so nicht möglich.
Töpfe und Pfannen waren nicht zu gebrauchen, so kaputt und dreckig wären sie allenfalls was für den Müll.
Auch Tassen, Teller und Besteck mussten erst von uns gespült werden, um letzte Reste von Nahrungsmitteln zu entfernen.
Mikrowelle und Ofen waren ebenfalls nicht richtig sauber und wurden daher nicht von uns genutzt.
Der Ventilator im Bad müsste auch dringend mal vom Dreck befreit werden, damit die Luft auch wirklich aus dem Raum heraus kann.
Leider gab es für beide WCs nur eine Bürste, die dann auch noch abgebrochen war.

Restaurant:
Von den Nudeln mit Bolognese würde ich abraten, da gibt es Gerichte aus der Dose, die besser schmecken.
Pizza war im Prinzip ok, allerdings könnte man eine Pizza auch so belegen, dass man  weniger als ca. 6cm Teigrand hat.
Dafür war der Burger recht gut.

Lage und Umgebung:
Bei "Sandy Bay" hätte ich mir einen schönen Sandstrand vorgestellt.
Einen Strand gibt es zwar, dieser ist aber für Menschen mit Beeinträchtigungen oder einem Kinderwagen kaum zu erreichen, da der Zugang über einen schmalen und recht steilen Weg führt.
Auch die Müllberge am Strand sollten mal entsorgt werden.
Wie schon beschrieben stehen die Mobilheime recht dicht beieinander, was dem Platz insgesamt den Charme einer städtischen Siedlung verleiht.

Insgesamt sind wir von den anderen Parkdean Ressorts vielleicht zu verwöhnt, aber vieles hier ging gar nicht.
Hier wäre schon viel gewonnen, wenn nach der Reinigung auch eine gewissenhafte Endkontrolle durchgeführt würde.

Noch einmal müssen wir das nicht haben.
